PAIN.quote: I'll admit it... I hate my driver, I hate everything about it. I struggle hitting the driver and have little confidence with it
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

PAIN.quote: It's the constant OB and lost balls that gets so boring
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

PAIN.quote: My driver is the bane of my life one day its on fire and makes scoring easy, however most of the time I battle round because my driving is gash
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

PAIN.quote: I literally have no idea if it's going to go straight, right or left
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

PAIN.quote: lessons, shorter driver shaft, less loft, more loft, different shaft, 3 wood, hybrid, 3 iron, 4 iron 5 iron you name it I'll try and have tried anything and everything
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

PAIN.quote: if tomorrow was a friendly hit I expect I'd hit 8 or 10 drivers in a round, if it was a medal I'd be amazed if I hit more than a handful and might not hit it at all
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

PAIN.quote: I really am struggling with driver and have done since coming back to the game last summer. I currently don't hit anything longer than a 5 iron off the tee
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

PAIN.quote: I used to have a real love hate relationship with mine. 3 of 13 drives would be blinders, long and straight and 10 would be a horror show
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

PAIN.quote: I used to struggle with a big slice. Got rid of my driver
PAIN.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

PAIN.quote: Same problem, I can hit my irons dead straight consistently, but I have a terrible slice with my driver that I just can't get rid of
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - I can't hit my driver it doesn't make sense

PAIN.quote: I can't hit my driver. It doesn't make sense!
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - I can't hit my driver thread title
GolfWRX - Zero confidence in driver thread title
PAIN.context: Thread dedicated to driver confidence crisis

PAIN.quote: I had a season where I couldn't hit driver and keep it on the golf course

HOPE.quote: I went through a few and could barely control any of them. Each tee shot was random. My current one is solid though
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

HOPE.quote: I decided to purchase a slightly shorter shaft and although I've only played 2 rounds with it I've been so much more consistent and haven't lost any noticeable distance
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle
HOPE.context: Shorter shaft = consistency without distance loss

HOPE.quote: My days of 3 off the tee are largely gone
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle
HOPE.context: No more penalty strokes from driver

HOPE.quote: You have to look down at your driver and have confidence. You don't so get rid of it
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

HOPE.quote: my driver now is by far the most favourite club in the bag. Missed 1 fairway last time out
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle
HOPE.context: Transformation from hate to favorite club

HOPE.quote: I now make sure I swing from the inside with my driver. I have done this for about a year and it performs brilliantly. I can't remember the last time I hit a big slice
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

HOPE.quote: I struggled badly with the driver for the first half of last year. So I bought a different driver. Problem solved, I love it now
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

HOPE.quote: I massively struggled with my 10.5 driver. I changed it to a 12 degree driver with a 55ish gram shaft. I found the lighter shaft and the more loft very useful. I now hit them well
HOPE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

HOPE.quote: Conquered my driver slice by slowing down. How did you solve yours?
HOPE.source: Reddit r/golf - Conquered my driver slice

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The driver is the most unforgiving club in the bag due to its lower loft and longer shaft. Lower loft creates more sidespin when the ball is cut
ROOT_CAUSE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Generally I'll play 3 wood or 2 iron off the tee, which works much better, but I feel like I'm giving up yards
ROOT_CAUSE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: A draw is nice but when it goes wrong the ball can go anywhere, Big Block right or pulls and Hooks
ROOT_CAUSE.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Sliced every single driver shot until I just slowed down - way down. And I tried everything, from grip changes, to weight shifting exercises, to ball placement
ROOT_CAUSE.source: Reddit r/golf - Conquered my driver slice
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Exhaustive list of slice fixes tried

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I've put a slightly shorter shaft in driver that is minimising the risks
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I forgot everything I had been told, went to the range and just kept making adjustments regardless of how it looked and felt. Gripping down definitely helped a lot
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Anyone who plays with me now will say I play it way too far forward and I have no doubt it's essentially a patch on a patch on a patch
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I purchased a mini driver which I had some success with then felt a bit more confident so bought a new driver and got it shortened
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: My pro now has me hitting a fade off the tee, its been a revelation. one side of the fairway taken out of play
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle

VILLAIN.quote: Learn to play out of the rough, trees etc. It's worked for me
VILLAIN.source: Golf Monthly Forums - The dreaded driver those who struggle
VILLAIN.context: Cynical acceptance of poor driving - recovery over prevention

high_value_themes_identified: Driver hatred is common and emotionally intense
Equipment changes provide real hope and results
